title: Research Memory Tools
business_process: Before a researcher runs a hypothesis, check the org's own graph — including abandoned attempts — and report only the internal sources searched. General literature research remains with the host agent.

acceptance_criteria.text: The MCP server starts, registers all five tools, and the host lists them.
The status and edge vocabularies in the tool contracts equal those in the live database DDL.
Duplication output is derived only from the internal graph and carries no external literature result field.
Two phrasings of the same hypothesis return UI verdict 'match'; an unrelated hypothesis returns 'open'.
A query related to the abandoned finding F-093 returns it, flagged abandoned, with its reason text attached and not ranked below confirmed work for being abandoned.
No tool output ever contains the word 'novel'.
A finding written through write_finding passes the identical validation as one written through the reviewed-write path — the human gate covers both.
A LUSC question about a signature previously tested in LUAD recalls the LUAD finding.
The MCP has no external literature client, cache table, or literature result field.
The rendered wiki page cites the findings it was generated from and is reproducible from the graph alone.
A biologist confirms that a recalled match is a genuine scientific match — it must survive being read aloud on stage.
